purpose: Visual scene plan, style cue, character refs, and image prompt controls for the Pallas Species Strikes branch-and-fold IF fixture.

visual_scene_plan.purpose: Illustrated Ink replay segmentation for website and imagegen use. Each entry maps a click-through Ink section or branch location to an imagegen-ready visual anchor.
visual_scene_plan.segmentation_rule: Distinct places, focal areas, object closeups, arrivals, legal/authority reframes, red-alert beats, branch locations, and aftermath states receive visual_scene_id anchors. If a new actor or institution changes the room's power geometry, stage the pre-arrival beat first, then give the arrival/reframe its own click-through and visual transition. Pivotal one-sentence beats may stand alone.
visual_scene_plan.prompting_rule: Prompts describe visible geometry, bodies, tools, lighting, material state, and stance as concrete drawable targets for the image model.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Wide establishing shot inside an AU Bloom shell maintenance yard before shift bell: a curved service lane built into the engineered inner shell of a rotating Bloom habitat, with consolidated asteroid aggregate and dark TCS substrate visible behind ribbed metal supports, scuffed deck plates bolted to the shell curve, overhead amber work lamps, wall-mounted pressure gauges, sleeping maintenance carts, folded work-support frames, cable trays, worn yellow-white safety stripes, faint industrial haze; empty pre-shift work infrastructure fills the vacant service lane; the infrastructure looks old, hand-repaired, scuffed, patched, and tired.

visible_characters.default_stance: environmental establishing shot with empty work infrastructure before workers arrive

visual_continuity_notes: Service Ring Kappa remains the same bounded local service loop around a dense Kappa seal-air-thermal equipment cluster: loop-inner manifold faces and access ports, loop-outer worker staging with loop-outer anchor rail hardpoints, cross-lane lockout rigging, far-loop lockers and break ledges, curved engineered Bloom shell, consolidated asteroid aggregate and dark TCS substrate, ribbed supports, worn yellow-white safety paint, hard industrial work lights, gray engineered-worker suits, and a partial AU supervisor catwalk/glassed operations gallery overlooking the primary manifold segment but not the whole loop. The frame favors constructed habitat infrastructure: shell plating, equipment-cluster machinery, seal hardware, pipework, manifold faces, deck plates, and service systems.
Use named-character visual refs for every visible named character. Preserve body type, clothing language, equipment, silhouette, and stable visual identity between scenes.
Use this as the broad location anchor for the fixture.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Wide-to-medium view down Service Ring Kappa, a curved local service-loop segment built into the engineered inner shell of a rotating Bloom habitat: on the left, dry-operation cephalopod work bays hold suspended dry-operation work-support frames with humidity rings, oxygenation tubes, flexible support loops, rounded valve clusters, and radial tool rails; on the right, a loop-outer baseline anchor rail of bolted steel track, anchor posts, heavy hooks, route bolts, and clipped safety tethers supports baseline human anchor riggers in patched brown-orange work jackets, heavy gloves, magnetic boots, and tool belts. Along the consolidated asteroid aggregate and TCS substrate wall, a glowing amber loop-inner manifold face panel spreads into pipe trunks, cable bundles, gauges, and route-diagram screens; engineered seal technicians in clean numbered gray skinsuits with small VitaForge BioDrone Standard batch tags, close-cropped hair, and precise identical work posture wait near it while Lio walks through the center carrying a slate.

visible_characters.default_stance: walking the center lane with slate tucked under one arm, tired but attentive
near the amber loop-inner manifold face with other numbered gray-skinsuit seal technicians
at the loop-outer anchor rail with a heavy hook and guarded posture
partly visible in a compact suspended dry-operation work-support rig at dry-operation work-support equipment

scenes.base_image_prompt: Medium shot at the loop-inner Kappa manifold face: a waist-high mechanical spine of ribbed pipe trunks, bundled fiber cables, removable patch plates, amber route-diagram screens, pressure gauges, warning glyphs, and small hand-sized diagnostic touch panels bolted into curved Bloom shell plating over consolidated asteroid aggregate and dark TCS substrate. Nara-7 touches one ribbed metal access panel twice, pauses, then touches it with two fingers spread; three engineered seal technicians stand behind her in a precise line, wearing clean numbered gray skinsuits with small VitaForge BioDrone Standard batch tags, close-cropped practical hair, pale gray-brown engineered-worker skin tones, and identical still work posture; Lio watches from the edge of frame with a slate.

watching from nearby as a quiet observer

scenes.base_image_prompt: Medium shot of the loop-outer baseline anchor rail: a long bolted steel track runs along the right side of the curved local service-loop segment, fitted with anchor posts, route bolts, heavy hooks, clipped safety tethers, scratched warning labels, and old tool lockers. Orrin Dax braces a heavy dark anchor hook across his knees while his baseline anchor crew clears bolts along the rail; the crew wears patched brown-orange work jackets, heavy gloves, magnetic boots, frost-worn sleeves, tool belts, and tired human faces lit by amber work lamps. Cheap recycler coffee mugs and patched gloves sit on a narrow fold-out break ledge; across the lane, engineered workers appear as distant clean gray figures near amber loop-inner manifold panels.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Medium shot of a cephalopod bare-minimum dry-operation work-support station in low gravity: a padded dry-operation work-support frame with humidity ring is bolted to the Bloom cavity service shell, surrounded by transparent oxygenation tubes, padded contact bands, rounded valve clusters, flexible radial tool rails, small diagnostic screens, and life-support lung diagrams painted on a nearby panel. Teth Inkwise floats in the work-support rig while several flexible tentacles manage valves and tools; one padded contact band has thin padding and leaves a pale rub mark on the mantle. Teth's posture is controlled and stoic; the station is heavily patched, carefully maintained, sparse, industrial, and visibly built around productivity, access, and survival margins.

visible_characters.default_stance: suspended in the work-support rig, several flexible tentacles managing valves and tools
standing beside the work-support rig checking the padded contact band

A visible badge-only stair drops from the catwalk to a lower landing at the primary Kappa-7 work segment, making her route from gallery to floor explicit.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Object-focused shot of Lio's slate held outward in front of the loop-inner Kappa manifold face, a ribbed pipe-and-cable service spine with bronze-gray trunks, amber route screens, pressure gauges, warning glyphs, and hand-sized touch panels blurred behind the display. Nara-7's hand hovers just above the display after marking it; Ilya and a supplier-liability window are visible beyond the slate but slightly out of focus; Orrin stands at the loop-outer baseline anchor rail edge of frame with his old anchor hook lowered, publicly backing the evidence from worker expertise.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Triptych-style final callback image with three clearly separated panels inside Service Ring Kappa, not one continuous physical alcove. Left panel: far-loop break-rack alcove with curved engineered Bloom shell plating, amber industrial work light, scuffed deck plates, cable trays, route bolts, and Orrin's battered recycler coffee mug with a fresh hairline crack sitting on a narrow metal shelf beside patched gloves. Center panel: Teth's dry-operation work-support station near the primary work segment, showing a repaired padded contact band beside transparent oxygenation tubes, open support loops, humidity ring, and compact tool rails. Right panel: primary Kappa-7 loop-inner manifold face with ribbed pipe trunks, patch plates, amber route screen, pressure gauges, and Nara's hand performing the twice-pause-third touch against the access panel while workers watch from a respectful distance. Thin blueprint divider lines and matching amber work lights connect the panels as memory and aftermath, while preserving that the far-loop break rack and Kappa-7 manifold are separate locations around the same service loop.

visual_scene_plan.global_style_cue: Style: Media Molecule Dreams-style 3D volumetric brushstroke painting; visible sculpted fleck strokes; painterly VR-made geometry; every detail is made of brush matter that feels partly solid, partly liquid, partly gas. Extremely detailed but soft, handmade, and non-photoreal. Tiny brushstrokes are allowed and encouraged, but the broad composition must remain readable.
visual_scene_plan.prompt_assembly_rule: Assemble each image prompt from visual_scene_plan.global_style_cue, visual refs only for characters actually visible in the rendered frame, the scene base_image_prompt, triggered state_image_modifiers, and triggered global_branch_image_modifiers whose apply_to_visual_scene_ids includes the current visual_scene_id. Use visual_continuity_notes only as tooling/review constraints; assembled image prompts use concrete visible scene, character, and modifier text.
review_notes: Imagegen spot check showed stronger ensemble fidelity than BFL but pushed Teth's work-support rig toward containment-chair imagery; visual prompts now favor open work-support frames, padded contact bands, support loops, humidity rings, and tool access while preserving sparse AU comfort.

visual_character_refs.purpose: Stable named-character visual identities for prompt assembly. Image prompts include these refs for every named visible character because stable recurring character appearance comes from visual_character_refs.
visual_character_refs.prompt_assembly_rule: For each visual_scene_plan scene, include the visual_character_refs for named characters actually visible in that rendered frame. Omit refs for characters outside the frame, merely mentioned, or represented only by objects.

lio_vale.species_or_body: baseline human adult from Belt labor housing
lio_vale.stable_visual_description: Lean, compact adult human with medium-brown skin, short black hair clipped close at the sides, tired dark eyes, and a scheduler's stillness that reads as careful and controlled. Wears a worn slate-gray AU maintenance jacket over a practical work harness, soft-soled magnetic boots, a utility belt with tags and seals, and carries a rectangular work slate with scuffed edges.
lio_vale.silhouette_notes: Usually centered between groups, shoulders slightly forward, one hand near the slate; visually reads as a tired worker-organizer with practical shop-floor presence.

nara_7.species_or_body: engineered humanoid seal technician, VitaForge BioDrone Standard labor line
nara_7.stable_visual_description: Slender engineered humanoid worker with smooth androgynous features, close-cropped dark hair, pale gray-brown skin with faint subdermal route-interface lines at wrists and neck, and a clean gray numbered seal-technician skinsuit with a small VitaForge BioDrone Standard batch tag. Movements are precise and economical, hands often flat against rails or wall panels.
nara_7.silhouette_notes: Still, narrow, and controlled; frequently framed by the manifold or other clean numbered gray-skinsuit engineered technicians.
do_not_change: faint wrist and neck interface lines

orrin_dax.stable_visual_description: Broad-shouldered baseline human rigger in his late working years, light-brown weathered skin, close-cropped graying hair, rough stubble, and a pale frost scar along one side of his jaw. Wears a patched brown-and-orange anchor-crew work jacket, heavy gloves, magnetic boots, and usually carries or rests near an old dark metal anchor hook and battered recycler coffee mug.
orrin_dax.silhouette_notes: Solid, guarded, slightly hunched from years of rail work; visually tied to anchor hooks, coffee, frost scars, and old tools.

teth_inkwise.species_or_body: uplifted cephalopod dry-operation shell-artery maintenance specialist
teth_inkwise.stable_visual_description: Large uplifted cephalopod worker with mottled blue-gray skin, expressive dark eyes, and multiple flexible cephalopod tentacles supported in a compact low-gravity dry-operation work-support station with a bare-minimum humidity ring and oxygenation loop. The rig uses padded contact bands, adjustable body-support loops, utility interlock socket, tool loops, and an open body-support frame as work-support equipment; oxygenation tubes, valve clusters, padded supports, and curved tool rails make the body positioned for work in dry cramped AU maintenance spaces with sparse industrial comfort. One padded contact band is visibly worn and leaves a pale rub mark, while Teth's controlled posture suggests stoic endurance under visible strain.
teth_inkwise.silhouette_notes: Nonhuman horizontal or suspended silhouette inside dry-operation work-support equipment, body supported by a low-gravity work-support rig with tentacles arranged around valves and tool rails.
do_not_change: padded contact bands and oxygenation loop
cephalopod body plan supported horizontally in a work-support rig

ilya_marne.stable_visual_description: Tall baseline human woman with cool light skin, severe silver-blonde hair pinned tight, sharp cheekbones, and a controlled corporate expression. Wears a clean dark AU superintendent coat with pale blue trim, polished magnetic boots, and a slim supervisor console cuff; often framed on an open supervisor catwalk with blue-white interface reflections.
ilya_marne.silhouette_notes: Vertical, polished, controlled, visually associated with an open supervisor catwalk, transparent waist-high safety rail, blue-white UI, and distance from the dirty floor.
do_not_change: dark AU superintendent coat with pale blue trim
framed by open catwalk rail and UI reflections
